label: The Oathcutter
description: A broad-beamed sailing ship built for contested waters, its tar-dark hull reinforced with iron strakes and stitched with heavy pitch seams. The deck carries a low, armored forecastle and a compact sterncastle whose shuttered ports protect charts, seals, and ledgers against spray and seizure. Lawmarks and registry plaques are bolted to the mainmast partners, asserting licensure and jurisdiction in a maritime frontier where authority is routinely challenged. A run of warding nails and salt-caked sigils lines the rails, treated as standard hazard protocol in waters subject to liminal breach and interdiction.
